title: Axel Martinez
text:
I’m a Financial Economics student at BYU-Idaho with minors in Accounting and Data Science, focused on the intersection of capital markets and analytics. I’m bilingual (English/Spanish), Venezuelan-born, and preparing for a career in markets and corporate finance in South Florida.
Outside the classroom, I lead a team of student consultants in BYU-Idaho’s Faculty Development office, where I built the program’s consultation metrics system from the ground up — Excel dashboards, turnaround-time analytics, and semester reporting used by program leadership.
I’ve also worked in consultative sales in Santiago, Chile, and served a two-year mission in Viña del Mar — experiences that made me comfortable working across languages and cultures (I speak English, Spanish, and Portuguese).
I like problems where finance and data meet: valuing companies with Bloomberg, cleaning and analyzing data, and building tools that make decisions faster.

title: Projects
text: A selection of personal work at the intersection of finance, data, and building useful things. (Course assignments are intentionally excluded.)

title: Projects
text:
Venezuela Human Development Map
An interactive choropleth of HDI across Venezuela’s federal entities, built in R with Leaflet using public census and development data. Motivated by wanting a clearer picture of regional inequality in my home country.
Tools: R, Leaflet, tidyverse, Quarto View project → (link your repo or rendered page here)

title: Projects
section: Para Mi Amor — Spanish Learning App
text:
Para Mi Amor — Spanish Learning App
A Duolingo-style interactive web app I designed and built from scratch as a gift — custom lessons, progress tracking, and gamified review. A fun exercise in product thinking, UX, and front-end logic.
Tools: HTML/CSS/JavaScript, React View project →

title: Projects
text:
Bloomberg Sector Analysis: U.S. Utilities
Independent deep-dive on NextEra Energy and the utilities sector using Bloomberg Terminal — comparable-company analysis, beta and analyst-recommendation screens, relative valuation (EQRV), and market-concentration (HHI) work.
Tools: Bloomberg Terminal, Excel Read the write-up →

More coming: I’m currently building out additional markets and data projects. Check back soon or find me on GitHub.
